query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "gatemem: benchmarking memory governance in multi-principal shared-memo" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) as it measures the performance and governance mechanisms (specifically page migration control and cost-benefit analysis) of multi-tenant shared memory systems, which is the core domain and mechanism of the user's question regarding "Memory Governance in Multi-Principal Shared-Memory." Additionally, it serves as an empirical baseline (c) for the specific problem of migration overhead in tiered memory, a critical component of memory governance benchmarks.
